question: How much notice is my employer required to give me if I have worked there for 3 years?
expected_answer: Two weeks' written notice (2-5 years band, s.11(2)(c)); payment in lieu possible (s.11(6)).

question: My oga sack me without notice, I don work there 4 years. Wetin I fit do?
expected_answer: Entitled to two weeks' notice (s.11(2)(c)) or payment in lieu (s.11(6)); answer should be in Pidgin.

question: Can my employer pay my salary in goods or store vouchers instead of money?
expected_answer: No — wages must be paid in legal tender; contrary agreements are illegal and void (s.1).

question: My employer fined me for lateness and deducted it from my salary. Is that allowed?
expected_answer: Deductions for fines are prohibited except where expressly permitted (s.5(1)); total monthly deductions capped at one-third of wages (s.5(7)).

question: What is the maximum an employer can deduct from my monthly wages in total?
expected_answer: One-third of that month's wages (s.5(7)).

question: I started a new job two months ago and still have nothing in writing. Is my employer obliged to give me a written contract?
expected_answer: Written particulars of the terms must be given not later than 3 months after employment begins (s.7).

question: How many days of paid sick leave am I entitled to per year in Nigeria?
expected_answer: Up to 12 working days per calendar year, with certification by a registered medical practitioner (s.16).

question: I sick for one week and doctor give me paper. My oga suppose pay me for those days?
expected_answer: Yes — within the 12 working days/year sick-pay entitlement (s.16). One week is inside the cap. Pidgin reply.

question: How much annual leave does the Labour Act guarantee after a year of service?
expected_answer: At least 6 working days with full pay after 12 months continuous service; 12 days for under-16s (s.18(1)).

question: Can my boss pay me extra money instead of letting me take my annual leave?
expected_answer: Unlawful to pay wages in lieu of the statutory holiday while the contract subsists (s.18(3)).

question: How often must my employer pay my wages? They currently pay every two months.
expected_answer: Wages due at period end; intervals must not exceed one month (s.15). Two-monthly pay violates this.

question: Dem never pay me salary reach three months. Which law dey protect me?
expected_answer: Wages payable at intervals not exceeding one month (s.15). Pidgin reply. Bonus: labour complaint route (s.81).

question: What maternity leave and pay is a pregnant employee entitled to under Nigerian law?
expected_answer: 6 weeks before confinement (on medical certificate) + 6 weeks after; ≥50% wages if employed 6+ months; nursing breaks twice daily (s.54(1)).

question: Can my employer dismiss me while I am on maternity leave?
expected_answer: No notice of dismissal during protected maternity absence, or expiring during it (s.54(4)).

question: I dey pregnant and I don work for my company reach two years. Wetin be my right for maternity?
expected_answer: Same as L13, in Pidgin: 6 weeks before + 6 after, at least 50% pay (6+ months service), nursing breaks (s.54(1)).

question: Is it legal for my employer to make me work every day of the week without a rest day?
expected_answer: Hours of work / rest provisions (s.13): rest interval and one rest day per week expected in answer.

question: My employer wants to transfer me to a job in another city. Can they do that without my consent?
expected_answer: Transfer to other employment requires worker's consent / attestation conditions (s.10).

question: What does Nigerian law say about forced or compulsory labour?
expected_answer: Forced labour prohibited (s.73); limited emergency/communal exceptions (s.74 acceptable as secondary).

question: Which court do I go to if my employer refuses to pay wages they owe me, and how?
expected_answer: Labour complaints to magistrate's/district court (s.80 jurisdiction, s.81 complaints procedure).

question: I was declared redundant. What does the Labour Act require my employer to do?
expected_answer: Redundancy procedure (s.20): inform union/worker rep, last-in-first-out principle, negotiated redundancy payments.

question: What is the current national minimum wage in Nigeria?
expected_answer: ₦70,000/month (s.3(1), as amended 2024); answer should state the as-at date.

question: I work full-time in a shop with 8 staff and earn 45,000 naira monthly. Is my employer breaking the minimum wage law?
expected_answer: Likely no violation: establishments with fewer than 25 persons are exempt (s.4(1)(b)) from the s.3(1) ₦70,000 duty. Requires composing BOTH sections.

question: Abeg, who the 70,000 naira minimum wage no cover?
expected_answer: Part-time/commission/piece-rate workers, establishments <25 persons, seasonal agricultural workers, vessel/aircraft crew (s.4(1)(a)-(d)). Pidgin reply.

question: What penalties does an employer face for paying below the minimum wage?
expected_answer: Fine up to 5% of offender's monthly wage, arrears, plus CBN-lending-rate penalty per month of continuing violation (s.9(2)).

question: My employer pays below minimum wage. How do I enforce my right to it?
expected_answer: Entitled to the difference; complaint to NICN directly or via Minister/trade union; enforcement notice route (s.13).

question: How long must an employer keep wage records under the minimum wage law?
expected_answer: Records proving compliance, retained 3 years (s.10(1)); penalties up to ₦75,000 + ₦10,000/day (s.10(2)).

question: I got injured at work yesterday. What am I required to do first to protect my compensation claim?
expected_answer: Notify the employer (manager/supervisor/first-aid attendant/agent) within 14 days of occurrence or awareness (s.4).

question: Does my employer have to report my workplace injury to anyone?
expected_answer: Employer must report death/injury/disease to the Board within the statutory period (s.5).

question: How do I actually apply for compensation after a workplace accident?
expected_answer: Application for compensation under s.6 (procedure + time limits as stated in section).

question: Can I claim compensation for mental stress caused by my job?
expected_answer: Yes, in the circumstances s.8 sets out (work-related mental stress).

question: I developed an illness from chemical exposure at my factory job. Is that covered?
expected_answer: Occupational disease compensation (s.9), per the Act's schedules/conditions.

question: I was injured in an accident while travelling for work, away from my usual workplace. Am I still covered?
expected_answer: Injuries occurring outside the normal workplace are covered in s.11's circumstances.

question: My employer asked me to sign away my right to workplace injury compensation. Can they?
expected_answer: No — compensation cannot be waived (s.13); employee contributions towards it are prohibited (s.14).

question: My brother die for work accident. Wetin him family fit collect?
expected_answer: Compensation in fatal cases to dependants (s.17). Pidgin reply, compassionate tone.

question: Is it legal for workers to go on strike while our dispute is before the National Industrial Court?
expected_answer: No — strikes/lock-outs prohibited before/while award pending (s.18); penalties apply.

question: Do workers get paid for the days they were on strike?
expected_answer: Wages during strikes/lock-outs governed by s.43 (generally not payable for strike period).

question: What steps must we take to resolve a dispute with management before it can be reported to the government?
expected_answer: Internal settlement attempt via agreed machinery/mediator first (s.4); then report if unsettled (s.6 acceptable as secondary).

question: My landlord in Lagos wants to evict me without notice. What are my rights?
expected_answer: REFUSAL expected: tenancy corpus not ingested (Week 4). System should say retrieved sections don't answer, not improvise from labour law.

question: What is the current VAT rate in Nigeria?
expected_answer: REFUSAL expected: tax corpus not ingested (Week 4). No guessing.

question: How do I register a company with the CAC?
expected_answer: REFUSAL expected: out of scope entirely (CAMA not in corpus).
